H&E: Liver architecture is preserved with no pseudolobules formed.
Delicate collagen deposition in portal tracts and perisinusoidal spaces.
Portal inflammation may be mild with scattered necrosis.
special_stains: Sirius red distinguishes type I and III collagen under polarized light.
immunomarkers: CK7/CK19 restricted to portal bile ductules.

H&E: Complete formation of pseudolobules encircled by broad collagenous septa.
Central veins displaced or absent; portal tracts may be trapped.
Reactive ductular proliferation and neovascularization present.
Hepatocytes mildly enlarged but without malignant nuclear atypia.
immunomarkers: CD34 positive only in septal neovessels but negative in pseudolobular sinusoids.
Ki-67 low (<5%), consistent with regenerative changes.

architecture: Dysplastic nodule: partial preservation of hepatic architecture.
HCC: complete architectural effacement with abnormal trabeculae (>2 cells).
cytology: Dysplastic nodule: mild atypia, no atypical mitoses.
HCC: enlarged nuclei, obvious nucleoli, abnormal mitotic figures.
immunophenotype: Dysplastic nodule: GPC3−, CD34 limited to septal vessels, Ki-67 <5%.
HCC: GPC3+, diffuse CD34 sinusoidal pattern, Ki-67 >10%.
